segments.text: HUGO: Hi Chantal. What did you think of the talk, then?
segments.translation: 雨果：你好，尚塔尔。你觉得这次讲座怎么样？

segments.text: CHANTAL: Hi Hugo. I thought it was good once I'd moved seats.
segments.translation: 尚塔尔：嗨，雨果。我移动了座位之后，觉得还不错。

segments.text: HUGO: Oh - were the people beside you chatting or something?
segments.translation: 雨果：哦，你旁边的人在聊天或者怎么了吗？

segments.text: CHANTAL: It wasn't that.
segments.translation: 尚塔尔：不是那样的。

segments.text: I went early so that I'd get a seat and not have to stand, but then this guy sat right in front of me and he was so tall!
segments.translation: 我去得早，这样我就可以有座位不用站着。但是有个人就坐在我前面，他太高了！

segments.text: HUGO: It's hard to see through people's heads, isn't it?
segments.translation: 雨果：头挡住什么也看不见，是吗？

segments.text: CHANTAL: Impossible! Anyway, to answer your question, I thought it was really interesting, especially what the speaker said about the job market.
segments.translation: 尚塔尔：绝不可能！无论如何，回答你刚才的问题，我认为这真的很有趣，尤其是演讲者所说的关于就业市场的内容。

segments.text: HUGO: Me too. I mean we know we're going into a really competitive field so it's obvious that we may struggle to get work.
segments.translation: 雨果：我也觉得。我的意思是我们知道我们将进入一个竞争非常激烈的领域，所以很明显我们可能很难找到工作。

segments.text: CHANTAL: That's right - and we know we can't all have that 'dream job'.
segments.translation: 尚塔尔：没错。我们知道我们不可能都拥有那份“梦想的工作”。

segments.text: HUGO: Yeah, but it looks like there's a whole range of … areas of work that we hadn't even thought of - like fashion journalism, for instance.
segments.translation: 雨果：是的，但看起来有很多工作领域我们甚至没有考虑过，比如时尚新闻。

segments.text: CHANTAL: Yeah - I wasn't expecting so many career options.
segments.translation: 尚塔尔：就是说。我没想到会有这么多职业选择。

segments.text: HUGO: Mmm. Overall, she had quite a strong message, didn't she?
segments.translation: 雨果：嗯。总的来说，她传达了一个非常强烈的信息，不是吗？

segments.text: CHANTAL: She did. She kept saying things like 'I know you all think this, but …' and then she'd tell us how it really is.
segments.translation: 尚塔尔：的确。她一直在说“我知道你们都这么想，但是……”。然后她会告诉我们真实情况是什么。

segments.text: HUGO: Perhaps she thinks students are a bit narrow-minded about the industry.
segments.translation: 雨果：也许她认为学生们对这个行业的看法有点狭隘。

segments.text: CHANTAL: It was a bit harsh, though! We know it's a tough industry.
segments.translation: 尚塔尔：但还是有点残酷！我们知道这是一个要求严格的行业。

segments.text: HUGO: Yeah - and we're only first years, after all. We've got a lot to learn.
segments.translation: 雨果：是的。毕竟我们才刚上一年级。我们有很多东西要学。

segments.text: CHANTAL: Exactly. Do you think our secondary-school education should have been more career-focused?
segments.translation: 尚塔尔：没错。你认为我们的中学教育应该更注重职业吗？

segments.text: HUGO: Well, we had numerous talks on careers, which was good, but none of them were very inspiring. They could have asked more people like today's speaker to talk to us.
segments.translation: 雨果：这个嘛，我们有很多关于职业的讲座，这很好，但没有一个是非常鼓舞人心的。他们本可以请更多像今天的演讲者这样的人给我们演讲。

segments.text: CHANTAL: I agree. We were told about lots of different careers - just when we needed to be, but not by the experts who really know stuff.
segments.translation: 尚塔尔：我同意。他们只是在我们需要的时候告诉我们关于许多不同职业的事情，但那些人不是真正了解职场的专家。

segments.text: HUGO: So did today's talk influence your thoughts on what career you'd like to take up in the future?
segments.translation: 雨果：那么今天的谈话是否影响了你对未来想从事什么职业的想法？

segments.text: CHANTAL: Well, I promised myself that I'd go through this course and keep an open mind till the end.
segments.translation: 尚塔尔：好吧，我答应过我自己会完成这门课程并保持开放的心态直到最后。

segments.text: HUGO: But I think it's better to pick an area of the industry now and then aim to get better and better at it.
segments.translation: 雨果：但我认为现在最好选择一个行业领域，然后致力于在这个领域做得越来越好。

segments.text: CHANTAL: Well, I think we'll just have to differ on that issue!
segments.translation: 尚塔尔：嗯，我想我们只能在这个问题上保持不同意见！

segments.text: HUGO: One thing's for certain, though.
segments.translation: 雨果：不过有一件事是肯定的。

segments.text: From what she said, we'll be unpaid assistants in the industry for quite a long time.
segments.translation: 按照她的说法，我们会作为助手在这个行业工作很长一段时间，但是没有薪水。

segments.text: CHANTAL: Mmm.
segments.translation: 尚塔尔：这个嘛。

segments.text: HUGO: I'm prepared for that, aren't you?
segments.translation: 雨果：我已经做好准备了，你呢？

segments.text: CHANTAL: Actually, I'm not going to accept that view.
segments.translation: 尚塔尔：实际上，我不打算接受这种说法。

segments.text: HUGO: Really? But she knows it's the case - and everyone else says the same.
segments.translation: 雨果：真的吗？但她知道情况确实如此，其他人也这么说。

segments.text: CHANTAL: That doesn't mean it has to be true for me.
segments.translation: 尚塔尔：这并不意味着对我也一定是这样的。

segments.text: HUGO: OK. Well - I hope you're right!
segments.translation: 雨果：好吧，我希望你是对的！

segments.text: CHANTAL: I thought the speaker's account of her first job was fascinating.
segments.translation: 尚塔尔：我认为演讲者对她第一份工作的描述很吸引人。

segments.text: HUGO: Yeah - she admitted she was lucky to get work being a personal dresser for a musician.
segments.translation: 雨果：是的。她说到她很幸运能找到一份给音乐家做私人梳妆师的工作。

segments.text: She didn't even apply for the job and there she was getting paid to choose all his clothes.
segments.translation: 她甚至没有申请这份工作，她被雇来挑选他所有的服装。

segments.text: CHANTAL: It must have felt amazing - though she said all she was looking for back then was experience, not financial reward.
segments.translation: 尚塔尔：一定感觉很棒，虽然她说她当时想要的只是经验，而不是经济回报。

segments.text: HUGO: Mmm. And then he was so mean, telling her she was more interested in her own appearance than his!
segments.translation: 雨果：嗯。然后他很刻薄，告诉她比起雇主，她对自己外表更感兴趣！

segments.text: CHANTAL: But - she did realise he was right about that, which really made me think.
segments.translation: 尚塔尔：但是，她确实意识到他是对的。这真的引发了我的思考。

segments.text: I'm always considering my own clothes but now I can see you should be focusing on your client!
segments.translation: 我一直在考虑自己的衣服，但现在我明白你应该关注你的客户！

segments.text: HUGO: She obviously regretted losing the job.
segments.translation: 雨果：她显然对失去这份工作感到遗憾。

segments.text: CHANTAL: Well, as she said, she should have hidden her negative feelings about him, but she didn't.
segments.translation: 尚塔尔：嗯，正如她所说，她应该隐藏对他的负面情绪，但她没有。

segments.text: HUGO: It was really brave the way she picked herself up and took that job in retail.
segments.translation: 雨果：她振作起来并接受了零售店的工作，这真的很勇敢。

segments.text: Fancy working in a shop after that!
segments.translation: 在那之后爱上了在商店工作！

segments.text: CHANTAL: Yeah - well, she recommended we all do it at some point.
segments.translation: 尚塔尔：是的。她建议所有人都可以在某个时间点去商店工作。

segments.text: I guess as a designer you'd get to find out some useful information, like how big or small the average shopper is.
segments.translation: 我想作为一名设计师，你会发现一些有用的信息，比如普通购物者的体型身材。

segments.text: HUGO: I think that's an issue for manufacturers, not designers.
segments.translation: 雨果：我认为这是制造者而不是设计师的事。

segments.text: However, it would be useful to know if there's a gap in the market - you know, an item that no one's stocking but that consumers are looking for.
segments.translation: 然而，这对了解市场是否存在缺口将很有用。你知道，有的商品没有人备货但消费者却都想要。

segments.text: CHANTAL: Yeah, people don't give up searching.
segments.translation: 尚塔尔：是的，人们会一直找。

segments.text: They also take things back to the store if they aren't right.
segments.translation: 如果人们没有买对东西也会退货。

segments.text: HUGO: Yeah. Imagine you worked in an expensive shop and you found out the garments sold there were being returned because they … fell apart in the wash!
segments.translation: 雨果：是的。想象一下，你在一家高档商店工作，你发现那里出售的衣服被退回了，因为它们在洗涤时破碎了！

segments.text: CHANTAL: Yeah, it would be good to know that kind of thing.
segments.translation: 尚塔尔：是的，能了解那些事会很好。

segments.text: HUGO: Yeah.
segments.translation: 雨果：是的。
